programmes.show_description: This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Dino-Kite. After Tish's kite gets stuck high up in a tree, Tash suggests they go and find a dinosaur to help them get it back.
Sandcastle. Children's animation. Bing and Flop build a sandcastle and Pando joins them in the sandpit with his digger toy. Bing finds a lovely 'poiky thing' in the sand.
Fast Slow, Fast Slow. Preschool series. The Teletubbies take turns to go up and down on the Dup Dup. Sometimes they go fast, sometimes they go slow - and even the Noo-noo has a go.
Dino Tee. Preschool animation about a baby monkey and his mother. Tee is so excited about a dinosaur party that he accidentally loses his voice from rawr-ing. Can Mo save the dino-day?
Dino Danger. A toy dinosaur escapes from its box and scares the Grape Kids.
Camping. Sula and Bing go camping to see the night sky. But then Bing sees a scary shadow...
Dino Dig. Mojo's got the perfect top for digging up the bones of a Swoppitus Rex. But could an actual dinosaur still be alive under Jackson's field, or is it a case of mistaken identity?
Calm Down. Water, water, everywhere - there's a flood in our hospital soap opera! Grown-up Tag the firefighter splashes around. It's a good job grown-up Happy the lifeguard is on hand.
The Taste Badge. Duggee is making a smoothie, and the Squirrels can't agree on whether it's yummy or not.
Musical Statues. Bluey and her family are out of sorts, so Mum gets everyone to play musical statues.
Handstand. At a party, Bingo is desperate to show off her handstand skills.
Go Jet Academy: Ghost Crabs. Glitch damages the Academy crabs' habitat when he takes all the sand from their beach.
The Tale of the Perilous Party. Animated series. When Peter discovers Mr Tod is the host of a surprise party, he must save the guests from becoming party food.
Hazardous Habitats. While collecting pictures, Dashi and Shellington get trapped in a flooding cave.
You're Five Ones and I'm Five Tens. Superstar Fifty sings a song that shows Five the patterns that make them similar in so many ways - if you can count to five, you can count to fifty!
Note. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. N, O, T and E make music - and S makes more and more of it!
Special Surprise Day. Love Monster has to use all his willpower not to open Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny's present before night-time - but what can it be?
Peas. Bella and Lift visit the Fortress of Fun, where Kingsley and Queenie have fun looking for peas with the Playtimers in their Pea Party game.
Leek. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Leek, who won't let anything, not even the chilly conditions backstage, stop him from singing his song Can't Hold Me Down.
Chalk Dinosaur. Children's animation. Bing draws a fabulous stripy, spotty, zig-zag dinosaur, but just as he has finished, a rain shower washes the chalk dinosaur away.
Dinosaur. Dee encourages a friend to say 'dinosaur', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. There's a stomping dinosaur, a drumming dinosaur and some disco dinosaurs!
Vacuumasaurus. Lu can't understand why Gus is afraid of the vacuum cleaner.
Brave Knight Rebecca. The Piggies build a castle so that Rebecca can be a knight and start an epic quest. But will everything go smoothly with Wilf as the dragon?
More About Outside. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos watch a hairdresser putting his signs up outside his shop and decide to put everything outside.
Dinosaurs. Justin and his friends go to a dinosaur park, while Mr Tumble visits Shopkeeper Tumble.
Ballet. Preschool series. Dipsy ballet twirls with the Tiddlytubbies, and the children in Tummy Tales put on a ballet show for their family at home.
Dinosaurs. Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear invite little ones and their grown-ups to explore dinosaurs through play, song and storytelling. Toddlers and grown-ups at home can join in too.
Train Station. Elsee meets her new friend Amara at the train station and plays a game of I-spy.
Waking Up. Aaron, Novah and Joda go on a springtime adventure, meeting a beatboxing daffodil to find out why all the plants are starting to wake up.
Ants! It's picnic day! Rebecca and Murphi create a 'soup-er' fly in a bowl of soup. On the Recycling Express, an old storage tub becomes a totally terrific tub drum kit.
Radishraptor Rumpus. Ginger and the Peas encounter a pack of Radishraptors who steal their delicious pop drops!
Daddy Robot. Bluey and Bingo don't want to tidy the playroom, so they get Daddy Robot to do it.
The Puddle Badge. Happy sees a very questionable looking puddle that he is determined to splash in.

programmes.show_name: Hey Duggee: Top of the Pups
programmes.show_description: Dinosaur Song. Chew Chew sings a song all about dinosaurs.
Whale Boat and Rockpool Walk. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers are on a walk to make faces in the sand. Along the way, they explore a rockpool and spot a heron.
George and Freddie Play Dino-Tennis. Mum and Dad challenge George and Freddie to an unusual game of dino-tennis.
Sebastiaan's South African Bobotie. Sebastiaan is making Bobotie with spiced mince meat, vegetables and an egg topping.

programmes.show_name: Teeny Tiny Creatures with Chantelle and Rory
programmes.show_description: Hedgehogs. Chantelle meets two hedgehog rescuers while Rory and Sandy are hedgehog spotting.
Mission: Fireworks. It's Fireworks Night, and therapy dog Toffee is helping his neighbour Tilly, who is a bit worried about all the loud bangs and whizzes.
Dinosaur. Nick and Norman get a visit from a giant T-Rex, and Norman does his best dinosaur roar.
Dinosaur Egg. Live-action children's show. Tim makes a nest for an egg and puts it in the airing cupboard in the hope that it will hatch into a baby dinosaur.
Stormy Weather. Oona and Baba get stuck on the cliff during a storm.
Dress Up. The potatoes dress up for a party and try on silly costumes.
The Beat in Your Feet. The garden gang compete in a three-legged race. Stripes struggles until he finds his three-legged rhythm.
The Party Badge. Animated preschool series. There's a parcel for Duggee, and it's wrapped up like a present. Maybe it's Duggee's birthday! The Squirrels decide to throw a party.
Lion. Preschool series about words. Dee encourages a friend to say 'lion', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure, seeing what lions look like and what they do.
We Need Another Hero. The heroes of the Eight Times Table are assembled by Octoblock to help save the day.
Ants. Educational fun. A, N and T sit down for a nice picnic, but soon everyone is getting tickled, the food starts disappearing and N gets the blame.
Autumn. Educational series using Makaton sign language. Justin has lots of autumn fun with his friends, but Mr Tumble can't play football because leaves are blocking the goal.
Keep Fit. Preschool comedy and education. The Teletubbies feel tired after doing lots of keep-fit exercises but they are ready for action again after eating lots of Tubby Custard.
Patchwork Anteater. Patchwork Anteater has a problem: his tongue is tied up in a knot!
Raa Raa and the Drip Drop Waterfall. Animation set in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. The waterfall has lost its tinkly tonk sound, so Raa Raa and his friends set out to find it.
Rock 'n' Rollers. Wilson, Koko and Brewster discover a hidden talent for music and form a band.
Episode 13. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Things get messy for Storybook Stan when he reads a story about a sunny tropical island.
Argentinosaurus and Eggshell. Preschool adventure series. Andy travels back 95 million years in search of an eggshell belonging to one of the biggest animals to ever walk the earth.
Stick to Me! The Game Catchers land on the Zen Garden Planet to play stick to me.
Duck-Billed Platypus. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his explorers. While rescuing river creatures after a storm, the Octonauts find a strange egg.
The Tale of the One That Got Away. Animated series. When Peter and friends encounter the legendary trout Jack Sharp, they try to reel the fish in once and for all.
The Tour Guide Badge. Animated preschool series. Gerbie the snake is on holiday and would like to see all the beautiful sights. Duggee can help - he does have his tour guide badge!
Fergie's Horrible Hiccups. When Fergie gets hiccups before her concert, Vida needs to come up with a preshow cure!
Supertato's Holiday. Supertato goes on holiday but can't relax because Evil Pea is sure to do something evil.
Mission Sleep. The ducklings are exhausted and just want to sleep, but Weasy starts snoring.
Turbo Ted. Ted the bus wants to be faster, and Mojo swops every top to try to make it happen. But it's Bo's homemade rocket that gives Ted the boost he needs... all the way into space!
Musical Statues. Bluey and her family are out of sorts, so Mum gets everyone to play musical statues.
Flutter's Fossil Find. The Piris find a skeleton buried in the ice and think it's an ancient ancestor. But after a series of strange discoveries, things take an unexpected turn.
Ice Cream. Bluey and Bingo try to taste each other's each ice creams, but they're starting to melt.
Andy and the Lost Pachycephalosaurus. Andy embarks on an adventure across Dino Island in search of a baby Pachycephalosaurus.
Waffle Learns to Read. Gramps teaches Waffle to read while Mrs Hobbs is dog-sitting Jelly next door.
The Lost and Found Wish. Rafi's lost toy ends up with Oscar, and she learns the joy of passing things on.
It's Time to Find a Dinosaur. JoJo and Gran Gran go to the museum to find a giant fossilised Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton.
Fluff Bread. Animation for preschool children. Sarah and Duck help Bread Man and Cake bake some very unique bread for Flamingo and John.
Hide-and-Seek. Stop-motion animation. Small, Tiny and Baby Soup Dragon have a race around the planet, but Baby Soup Dragon cheats so he can win.
Being MeMo. BaBa is playing a copying game, but MeMo wants to play something else. With the help of the Yummies, they find a happy way to compromise.
Funny Noises from the Pinky Ponk. Series for young children. The Tombliboos ride in the Pinky Ponk, enjoying their Pinky Ponk juice. They make a funny noise when they sip the juice.
Andy Day - Dinos Don't Give Up. Andy Day tells the story of Dinah the Diplodocus, who can do anything... except surf!
This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Diving for Marbles. Tish is excited about the marbles competition at school, but an accident with Bobby causes her mood to sour after the marbles scatter around the room.
Acorns. Children's animation. Bing and Sula collect acorns in the park with Flop. When Bing's pile of acorns keeps getting smaller, he and Flop try to discover the culprit.
Give It Back. Preschool series. Tinky Winky and Dipsy both want to use Tinky Winky's bag and start to squabble. The Noo-noo sucks up the bag until they are ready to share.
Best Day Ever. Tee and Mo are having the greatest day ever and Tee doesn't want it to end. Can Mo convince him to go to bed with the promise that tomorrow is going to be even better?
Wonder Pea. Evil Pea tricks Supertato by pretending to be his sidekick and captures him instead.
Bus Ride. The seaside bus breaks down, but Bing still finds a way to have fun.
Ice Cream Float. Betty's ice cream is lifting everyone off the ground. Mojo and Bo need to get them down.
Master of Puppets. At Squirrel Club, there's a story told in blocks, the tale of a first tooth, a fable about changing emotions, and a yarn involving the Squirrels as puppets.
The Biology Badge. Duggee and the Squirrels hop inside the Biology Bus and miniaturise themselves to go on a voyage of discovery inside Enid. Can they find out what's making her cough?
Stories. Indy's upset her model horse looks like a cow, but Calypso encourages her not to give up.
Road Trip. Bluey and Bingo find ways to entertain themselves on a family road trip.
The Blarney Stone, Ireland. Kyan finds he has to use some super smooth talking to save the tourists and protect Blarney Castle from Glitch's plan to get the gift of the gab.
The Tale of Benjamin's Blunder. Animated series. When Peter realises he has accidentally upset Benjamin, he goes to apologise, only to find his cousin has gone.
Iguazu Falls. The Octonauts must clear a logjam while protecting a family of birds.
The Forest of Tens. Thirty-Six and Forty-Five are exploring the Forest of Tens on a special quest. But what is the special something they need to find and bring to Ten?
Magic. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. Magic E makes mischief and demonstrates how to turn a cap into a cape.
Clear Out Day. Love Monster tries to share an old disco ball with his friends, but they all want to do different things with it.
Towel. Bella plays funny games with Lift using her colourful towel. Together, they visit Hettie in the Ice Disco, who uses a big towel to make up a new dance - the Butterfly Boogie!
Grapefruit. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Grapefruit dedicating the song All My Friends to the close pals who go with her everywhere.
Face Paint. Sula paints Bing to look like Biteysaurus, but upsets Bing by accidentally adding purple.
Ship. Dee encourages a friend to say 'ship', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We explore a pirate ship, a steering ship, a flapping flag ship and even meet a jumping ship!
Copy Copy Caterpillar. Lu gets frustrated when Gus won't join in her mimicking game.
Tele-Mag. Cesar invents the Tele-Mag, a gadget that lifts objects. But Wilf steals it to play tricks on the neighbours, causing lots of trouble!
More About Twirling. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos watch a gymnast dance whilst twirling a ribbon, then try to copy her dancing.
Animal Antics. Justin and his friend meet some amazing reptiles and make a snake sock puppet. Mr Tumble has his own animal fun when five speckled frogs come to visit.
Taking a Ride. Preschool series. The Teletubbies ride the Dup Dup and the Tubby Custard Machine. In Tummy Tales, a boy takes a ride on a cable car.
Cardboard Box. Giovanna and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore cardboard boxes through discovery, play, song and story. Babies and grown-ups at home can join in, too!
Back Garden. Elsee plays I-spy with her friend Bailey in his back garden.
Minibeasts. Aaron, Nelly and Adrian are on an adventure, looking for bugs in the city centre oasis, whilst finding out how minibeasts help the garden grow.
Hide-and-Seek. Murphi is so good at hiding that Rebecca decides to make a cardboard tube tracker dog. The Recycling Express brings a shoebox, which becomes home to a magnificent monster.
Egg Drop. Ginger and the Peas help a bokchoydactyl retrieve its egg from a nest of cucumberdiles.
Shadowlands. On a picnic in the park, Bluey and her friends play Shadowlands.
The Election Badge. When Eugene unexpectedly drops out of running the summer fair, Duggee and the Squirrels hold an election to choose the best candidate for the job.
Woolly Bench and Giant Spiderweb Walk. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers find a woolly bench and wash a giant greenhouse on a walk to catch a bus.

programmes.show_name: My First Festivals: St Patrick's Day
programmes.show_description: St Patrick's Day. We follow Memphis and his family as they learn about and celebrate the world-famous Irish festival of St Patrick's Day.
Charlotte's Irish Boxty. Charlotte makes Irish boxty for her friends using mashed and grated potato. She tops her boxty with cooked egg and serves it with tomato.

programmes.show_name: Teeny Tiny Creatures with Chantelle and Rory
programmes.show_description: Grasshoppers. Chantelle meets a stick insect, while Rory and Sandy are out listening for grasshoppers.
Mission: Birthday Surprise. Hearing dog Diesel helps Rayane with Mum's extra-special secret birthday surprise.
Giant Chinese Salamander and Alaotran Gentle Lemur. Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Chinese giant salamanders and Alaotran gentle lemurs.
Waffle is a Listening Dog. Waffle tries to impress on his first day as a listening dog at school. Mrs Hobbs arrives to collect Waffle, but her cat, George, goes missing.
Rock Music. Oona is nervous about her first dawn chorus and practises singing with Mama.
Science. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. The potatoes explore science in this new wave music video. From bugs to electricity, science is all around us!
Hiccups. When Toad finds himself with a case of puddle hiccups, it's up to Yukee and the rest of the garden gang to find a cure.
The Sailing Badge. Animated preschool series. A giant, regal sailing ship runs aground on the beach, so the Squirrels go to investigate.
Apple. Preschool series about words. Dee encourages a friend to say 'apple', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure, exploring what apples look and taste like.
Super Eights Assemble. Get to know the Eight Times Table and the Super Eights as they assemble in the museum of superheroes, ready to save the day.
Ink. Educational fun. When I finds some invisible ink, she plays all kinds of incredible tricks on the other Alphablocks, but soon wishes she hadn't!
Music Stars. Educational series using Makaton sign language. Justin and some musical friends put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent!
Party Invitation. Preschool series. A little girl makes birthday party invitations. The Teletubbies receive a magical party invitation from the Tiddlytubbies and do party dancing.
Patchwork Vulture. Patchwork Vulture has a problem: Ladybird loves to sing and dance, but he can't stand it! So how can they become friends?
Raa Raa and the Noisy Nap. Raa Raa and his friends are looking for a rumbly noise, while Crocky tries to find a quiet spot for a nap.
Odd Train Out. Zephie, Wilson and Brewster compete to be Koko's partner in a relay race.
Episode 14. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Arthur Sleep has missed the bus so Keith Fitt has a very important job to do: reading the news!
Woolly Mammoth and Tusk. Preschool adventure series. Andy arrives 30,000 years in the past, joins a herd of woolly mammoths and bumps into one of his own ancient ancestors.
The Leprephant and the Rainbow. The Game Catchers land on the Lucky Planet to play the cut-out re-assembly challenge.
Sea Skaters. Animated deep sea adventures with Captain Barnacles and his explorers. When Kwazii gets lost at sea aboard the tiny Octo-Ski, he meets a flotilla of sea skaters.
The Tale of the Runaway Kites. Animated series. Benjamin's up in the air as the unwilling passenger of a flyaway kite.
The Key Badge. Animated preschool series. Roly is digging in the garden when his spade suddenly hits something. It's a key. But what does it do?
Koa's Prickly Predicament. Vida helps Koa get over his fear of needles when a porcupine quill is removed. The key is distractions -  more specifically, a very funny joke from Popcorn!
Disco Fog. Evil Pea fogs up the supermarket by using a fog machine so that Supertato and the Tato Team get lost forever - but she ends up getting lost herself.
Mission Keepsakes. Can Weasy get his keepsakes back from the farmer's house?
Ready, Sheddy, Go. Nori and Dot are stuck in a treehouse without a door. How can Mojo and Bo get them out? By sending the treehouse flying across town, that's how!
Stories. Indy's upset her model horse looks like a cow, but Calypso encourages her not to give up.
Operation Seal. The Piris believe a seal colony is planning a takeover, but are their fears justified?
Dunny. Mum bans the girls from saying 'dunny', so they try and trick her into saying it instead.
Andy and the Quetzalcoatlus Egg. Andy embarks on an epic adventure across Dino Island to return a Quetzalcoatlus egg back to its nest and reunite it with its family before it hatches.
Waffle the Grand-Dog. Gram and Grand visit. Waffle loves Gram; he'll only leave his new dog doormat for her.
The Magic Show Wish. Rafi's magic messes with Finn's show, but teamwork helps him shine on his own.
It's Time for a Folk Tale. JoJo, Gran Gran and the Nature Club hear a special folk tale about a spider named Anansi.
Moon Bow. Animation for preschool children. Rainbow gets to experience the night for the first time.
Rainbow Star. Stop-motion animation. Small catches seven coloured stones floating in space. The Iron Chicken has also found a strange metal disc in space. Are they connected?
Spinning. MeMo and BaBa want to spin without stopping. On Planet BeddyByes, they discover many spinning things and learn why taking breaks can be useful.
Quiet Please Tombliboos, Upsy Daisy Wants to Sing. Series for young children. Upsy Daisy sets up her own special megaphone to sing but she can't make herself heard.
Geri Halliwell Horner - Rock 'n' Roll Soul. Rock out with Geri Halliwell-Horner in today's Bedtime Story.
